title: Agent tools — SpawnAgent, Fork, Task, Skill, RunScript, ToolSearch, ApplyPatch
purpose: Implement the tools that agents use to coordinate with other agents, manage tasks, activate skills, run scripts, search the tool catalog, and apply patches. Agent coordination tools (SpawnAgent, Fork, SendMessage/WaitAgent/CloseAgent) wrap the infrastructure from N-014. Standalone tools (Task, Skill, RunScript, ToolSearch, ApplyPatch) are self-contained and only depend on the Tool trait from N-006.
task: Implement 8 tools across 6 files in tools/: agent.rs (SpawnAgent, Fork, SendMessage, WaitAgent, CloseAgent — 5 tools wrapping N-014 infrastructure), task.rs (Task tool with CRUD ops), skill.rs (Skill tool loading prompt templates), script.rs (RunScript executing inline Rhai), tool_search.rs (ToolSearch with BM25), patch.rs (ApplyPatch applying unified diffs). All implement the Tool trait from N-006. Testing: Layer 1 unit tests.

requirements.title: SpawnAgent tool
requirements.spec: SpawnAgent tool SHALL create a sub-agent by calling AgentRegistry::reserve and then launching the agent loop. The tool returns when the sub-agent completes, providing its structured output.
acceptance: SpawnAgentTool implements Tool with name 'spawn_agent', effect Process
Input schema: { task: String, model: String, role: String, tools: Option<Vec<String>>, path: Option<String> }
Tool accesses AgentRegistry via Arc from the typed ToolContext extensions map [amended 2026-07-03: ToolContext.runtime_args was deleted by owner ruling — docs/DECISIONS-2026-07.md §4]
Calls registry.reserve(), spawns agent loop via tokio::spawn, returns SpawnGuard handle
Output includes agent_id, result (sub-agent's structured output), usage
Error when registry is not available in context
Test with MockProvider: spawn sub-agent, verify registry entry created and cleaned up

requirements.title: Fork tool
requirements.spec: Fork tool SHALL fork the current agent onto a different model for a bounded task, calling the fork logic from N-014, and return the ForkAudit as tool output.
acceptance: ForkTool implements Tool with name 'fork', effect Process
Input schema: { task: String, model: String, include_system: Option<bool>, include_recent_n: Option<u32>, exclude_tool_calls: Option<bool> }
Constructs ForkConfig from input and calls fork() from agent/fork.rs
Returns ForkAudit fields as structured JSON output
Test: fork with mock provider, verify ForkAudit returned with correct model

requirements.title: SendMessage, WaitAgent, CloseAgent tools
requirements.spec: Three coordination tools: SendMessage sends to another agent's mailbox, WaitAgent blocks until a specific agent completes, CloseAgent requests an agent to wrap up.
acceptance: SendMessageTool implements Tool with name 'send_message', effect Process
SendMessage input: { to: String, content: Value, trigger_turn: Option<bool> }
SendMessage calls Mailbox::send with the sender's agent ID from context
WaitAgentTool implements Tool with name 'wait_agent', effect Process
WaitAgent input: { agent_id: String, timeout: Option<u64> }
WaitAgent polls registry until agent status is Completed or Failed, with configurable timeout
CloseAgentTool implements Tool with name 'close_agent', effect Process
CloseAgent input: { agent_id: String, reason: Option<String> }
CloseAgent sets target agent status to Completing
Test: send message, verify it arrives in target mailbox

requirements.title: Task tool with CRUD operations
requirements.spec: Task tool SHALL support create, list, update, and complete operations on a task store. Tasks have status, description, dependencies, and metadata.
acceptance: TaskTool implements Tool with name 'task', effect ReadOnly for list, Write for create/update/complete
Input schema: { action: String, task_id: Option<String>, description: Option<String>, status: Option<String>, depends_on: Option<Vec<String>>, metadata: Option<Value> }
Actions: 'create', 'list', 'update', 'complete', 'get'
TaskEntry struct: id (String), description (String), status (TaskStatus), depends_on (Vec<String>), metadata (Value), created_at (DateTime<Utc>), updated_at (DateTime<Utc>)
TaskStatus enum: Pending, InProgress, Completed, Blocked
TaskStore trait abstracts storage — default InMemoryTaskStore provided
List action supports filtering by status
Test: create 3 tasks, list all, update one, complete one, verify states

requirements.title: Skill tool loading prompt templates
requirements.spec: Skill tool SHALL load a SKILL.md prompt template by name and inject its content into the agent's context. Skills are looked up from a configurable directory.
acceptance: SkillTool implements Tool with name 'skill', effect ReadOnly
Input schema: { name: String }
Searches skill_dirs (Vec<PathBuf> from context) for <name>/SKILL.md or <name>.md
Returns the skill content as the tool output for the model to follow
Returns clear error when skill not found, listing available skills
Test: create temp dir with a skill file, load by name, verify content returned

requirements.title: RunScript tool for inline Rhai execution
requirements.spec: RunScript tool SHALL execute inline Rhai scripts with sandboxed host functions. This is lightweight ad-hoc scripting, not heavy orchestration. Host functions split into blocking (read_file, run_cmd) and handle-returning (spawn_agent) categories per grilling refinement.
acceptance: RunScriptTool implements Tool with name 'run_script', effect Process
Input schema: { script: String, args: Option<Value> }
Creates a Rhai Engine with registered host functions
Blocking host functions: read_file(path) -> String, run_cmd(cmd) -> String, parse_json(s) -> Dynamic, to_json(val) -> String
Script args injected as a Rhai scope variable named 'args'
Script return value serialized to serde_json::Value via serde_json (StepOutcome bridge pattern)
Script execution has a configurable timeout (default 30s)
Cargo.toml adds rhai crate dependency
Test: run script that reads a temp file, verify output matches file content

requirements.title: ToolSearch with BM25 ranking
requirements.spec: ToolSearch tool SHALL perform BM25 keyword search over the tool catalog (name + description) and return ranked results. This is useful when large tool registries exist, especially MCP servers with many tools.
acceptance: ToolSearchTool implements Tool with name 'tool_search', effect ReadOnly
Input schema: { query: String, max_results: Option<u32> }
BM25 scoring computed over tool name and description text
Returns ranked list of { name, description, score } objects
Default max_results is 10
Empty query returns all tools sorted alphabetically
Test: register 5 tools with varied descriptions, search for keyword, verify top result is most relevant

requirements.title: ApplyPatch tool for unified diffs
requirements.spec: ApplyPatch tool SHALL apply unified diff patches to files. Post-validate runs AST validation via tree-sitter (same mechanism as Edit tool from N-010) using Gate semantics — if AST breaks, the patch is rolled back.
acceptance: ApplyPatchTool implements Tool with name 'apply_patch', effect Write
Input schema: { patch: String, working_dir: Option<String> }
Parses unified diff format (---/+++ headers, @@ hunks)
Applies hunks to target files, respecting context lines for positioning
post_validate_mode returns Gate (same as Edit tool)
Post-validate calls the same AST validation used by Edit (tree-sitter parse, check for ERROR nodes)
pre_validate checks all target files exist and have been read (same read-tracking as Edit)
Returns diff summary: files modified, hunks applied, lines added/removed
Test: create a file, apply a valid patch, verify file content changed correctly
Test: apply a patch with bad context lines, verify error returned

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement AgentRegistry, Mailbox, Fork logic, Monitor, or Goals — those are N-014
SHALL NOT implement tree-sitter grammar loading — reuse the grammar infrastructure from N-010
SHALL NOT implement heavy Rhai orchestration (workflow engine) — RunScript is lightweight ad-hoc only
SHALL NOT implement semantic/vector search in ToolSearch — BM25 keyword only
SHALL NOT implement handle-returning Rhai host functions (spawn_agent) — those require N-014 integration and are deferred to N-016
SHALL NOT add todo!() or unimplemented!() macros anywhere
verification: cargo test -p norn — all tools/ tests pass
No file over 500 lines of code (excluding tests, comments, whitespace)
tools/mod.rs contains only pub mod declarations and pub use re-exports
grep -rn 'unwrap()\|expect(' crates/norn/src/tools/ returns no matches outside test code
